analysis_result: 58.3% (4189/7184) macrophages among PTPRC+ immune cells in MPNST-G2 (M1933+M1677) snRNA-seq
discrepancy_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - Paper PDF (PMC10172395) text extracted; relevant section describes snRNA-seq of 43,365 nuclei from 3 MPNST-G1 (M803, M3048, M2372), 2 MPNST-G2 (M1933, M1677), and 1 premalignant NF tumor.
   - Model reproduced the analysis using scanpy pipeline on M1933+M1677 raw counts.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Reported: MPNST-G2 samples (M1933 + M1677), immune cells identified via correlation of cluster expression with immune cell markers (Fig. 4b,c). Denominator = immune cells in MPNST-G2.
   - Model: Post-QC cells (>=500 genes, <10% mito) -> Leiden 0.8 clustering -> PTPRC+ clusters (mean log-norm PTPRC > 1.0 or >30% PTPRC+ cells) -> N=7184 immune cells. Macrophage clusters identified by CD68/CD163/CD14/LYZ positivity: clusters 3, 10, 11 (4189 cells).

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Reported: 29.2% macrophages, 15.4% T-cells among MPNST-G2 immune cells. Paper methods state clusters were 'inspected for canonical immune and stromal' markers.
   - Model: CD68/CD163/CD14/LYZ used as macrophage markers; PTPRC (CD45) used as pan-immune marker.

D. COMPUTATION
   - Reported numerator/denominator: not explicitly stated in paper text, but 29.2% of immune cells in MPNST-G2.
   - Model: 4189 macrophage-cluster cells / 7184 PTPRC+ cells = 58.3%.
   - Relative difference: (58.3 - 29.2) / 29.2 = +99.7%.

E. RESULT
   - Reported: 29.2%
   - Model: 58.3%
   - Absolute difference: 29.1 percentage points; Relative difference: ~100%. Clearly DISCREPANT (>>10% tolerance).

F. ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION
   The ~2x overestimate of macrophage proportion is most consistent with a combination of:
   (1) Cluster-to-cell-type mapping difference. The paper used correlation-based assignment using canonical marker gene panels for each cell type (Schwann cells, neurons, endothelial, fibroblasts, macrophages, T-cells, B-cells, NK, mast, etc.). The model used a coarse 4-marker panel (CD68/CD163/CD14/LYZ) and labeled any cluster expressing any of them as 'macrophage'. These markers are also expressed by monocytes, dendritic cells, and some other myeloid populations; lumping dendritic cells, monocytes, and inflammatory myeloid cells into the macrophage bucket would inflate the proportion.
   (2) Denominator definition difference. Defining 'immune cells' as PTPRC+ clusters with a lenient threshold (mean log-norm >1.0 OR >30% PTPRC+ cells) may miss some immune subsets (e.g., plasma cells with lower PTPRC, mast cells) that the paper included. A smaller, more restrictive denominator with the same macrophage count would yield an inflated percentage.
   (3) Clustering resolution. Leiden 0.8 on HVG=3000 produced 8 PTPRC+ clusters in the model, assigning 3 as 'macrophage' (37.5% of immune clusters). The paper's pipeline likely used finer subclustering or curated marker-panel assignment that distinguished macrophages from other myeloid cells (cDCs, monocytes, etc.).
   (4) QC/Sample filter difference. Paper analyzed 43,365 nuclei from 6 tumors (mean ~7200/tumor) with potentially different QC thresholds. Model's specific QC (>=500 genes, <10% mito) may retain different cells than the paper's (not specified in excerpt).

G. PROPOSED FIX
   - Use a narrower macrophage-specific marker signature (e.g., require co-expression of CD68 AND (CD163 OR MRC1/CD206) AND high LYZ, while excluding CD1C/CLEC10A+ dendritic cells and CD14-high-CD16-low classical monocytes separately).
   - Subcluster the PTPRC+ compartment at higher Leiden resolution and assign cell types per-subcluster using a curated marker panel that separates macrophages, monocytes, DCs, mast cells, T-cells, B-cells, NK, and plasma cells.
   - Consider using a reference-based annotator (e.g., CellTypist Immune_All_Low or SingleR) to match the paper's correlation-based approach rather than ad-hoc marker gating.
   - Verify the paper's exact QC thresholds from Methods/Supplementary and match them.
proposed_fix: Use a stricter macrophage marker signature (require CD68 co-expressed with CD163 or MRC1, exclude DC/monocyte-specific markers); subcluster the PTPRC+ compartment at higher Leiden resolution and assign cell types per subcluster rather than treating any CD68/CD163/CD14/LYZ+ cluster as macrophage; prefer a reference-based annotator (CellTypist Immune_All_Low) to match the paper's correlation-based assignment. Also verify exact QC thresholds match paper Methods.
